summary:
Nitrates alleviate acute angina primarily through veno-arteriolar vasodilation, which decreases cardiac preload—reducing myocardial oxygen demand and wall stress. Despite affecting both preload and afterload, the predominant physiologically impactful mechanism linked to symptom relief is reduced preload due to improved ventricular filling dynamics.  

Note: Option A ("coronary vasodilation") was discounted because general systemic vasodilation predominates over specific coronary vessel relaxation; such an effect may worsen ischemia without adequate collaterals or perfusion matching.

Despite affecting both preload and afterload, the predominant physiologically impactful mechanism linked to symptom relief is reduced preload due to improved ventricular filling dynamics.  

Note: Option A ("coronary vasodilation") was discounted because general systemic vasodilation predominates over specific coronary vessel relaxation; such an effect may worsen ischemia without adequate collaterals or perfusion matching.. Answer choice: B
